description: A fully self-hosted, zero-trust Kubernetes platform. Clone, configure, run. Produces a complete production-grade development environment with GitOps, observability, testing, and AI-native project management.

stories.title: Scaffold repo structure and global Helm values
stories.description: Create the complete directory structure outlined in CLAUDE.md. Create a global values file at charts/_globals/values.yaml with domain, storageClass, and keycloak placeholders. Create a config.yaml.example that users fill in before running bootstrap. Create a minimal README.md explaining the project and prerequisites.

charts/_globals/values.yaml exists with global.domain, global.storageClass, global.keycloak stubs
config.yaml.example exists with comments explaining every field

stories.title: Bootstrap script: generic VPS and Hetzner provisioning
stories.description: Create bootstrap/bootstrap.sh as the main entry point that reads config.yaml and calls the appropriate provider script. Create bootstrap/providers/hetzner.sh using the Hetzner Cloud CLI (hcloud) to provision a CX21 or larger server, install K3s, and output kubeconfig. Create bootstrap/providers/generic-vps.sh that SSH-installs K3s on any Ubuntu 22.04 server given an IP and SSH key. Both must print estimated cost and Cloudflare wildcard DNS instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap/bootstrap.sh reads config.yaml and routes to correct provider
bootstrap/providers/hetzner.sh provisions server via hcloud CLI and outputs kubeconfig
bootstrap/providers/generic-vps.sh SSH-installs K3s on any Ubuntu 22.04 server
Both scripts print monthly cost estimate and Cloudflare DNS setup instructions

bootstrap/verify.sh checks that kubectl can connect and nodes are Ready

Create docs/providers/hetzner.md, docs/providers/aws-ec2-free-tier.md, docs/providers/digitalocean.md with step-by-step human instructions.

stories.title: Bootstrap: front door provider interface and VPS hardening framework
stories.description: Create a pluggable 'front door' security provider pattern for the bootstrap. Add bootstrap/frontdoor/interface.sh defining 5 shell function hooks every provider must implement: frontdoor_provision (local: create tunnel/service), frontdoor_install_agent (remote: install daemon on VPS), frontdoor_allowed_cidrs (returns newline-separated CIDRs to whitelist — everything else dropped), frontdoor_configure_dns (point DNS records), frontdoor_connection_info (print how to connect after bootstrap). Add bootstrap/frontdoor/none.sh as the baseline impl (allowed_cidrs prompts for caller IP, no agent, prints SSH instructions). Add hardening scripts that ALWAYS run regardless of front door: bootstrap/hardening/base.sh (unattended-upgrades, fail2ban, auditd, purge unused packages), bootstrap/hardening/ssh.sh (PasswordAuthentication no, PermitRootLogin prohibit-password, PubkeyAuthentication yes), bootstrap/hardening/kernel.sh (CIS benchmark sysctl: tcp_syncookies, rp_filter, dmesg_restrict, suid_dumpable=0), bootstrap/hardening/firewall.sh (UFW default-deny, reads frontdoor_allowed_cidrs output, opens only those CIDRs). Update bootstrap/config.yaml.example to add frontDoor field (default: cloudflare). Update bootstrap/bootstrap.sh to source the chosen front door impl and call hooks in order: harden → frontdoor_provision → frontdoor_install_agent (remote) → frontdoor_configure_dns → firewall.sh → frontdoor_connection_info.
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap/frontdoor/interface.sh documents all 5 required hooks with usage comments
bootstrap/frontdoor/none.sh implements all 5 hooks (allowed_cidrs prompts for caller IP)
bootstrap/hardening/base.sh installs and enables unattended-upgrades, fail2ban, auditd
bootstrap/hardening/ssh.sh disables password auth, root password login, enforces pubkey
bootstrap/hardening/kernel.sh applies CIS benchmark sysctl settings
bootstrap/hardening/firewall.sh calls frontdoor_allowed_cidrs and applies UFW rules
bootstrap/bootstrap.sh sources frontdoor impl and calls hooks in correct order
bootstrap/config.yaml.example has frontDoor field with cloudflare as default
shellcheck passes on all new .sh files

stories.title: Bootstrap: Cloudflare front door implementation
stories.description: Implement bootstrap/frontdoor/cloudflare.sh with all 5 interface hooks using Cloudflare Tunnel + Zero Trust Access (free tier). frontdoor_provision: use Cloudflare API (token + account ID from config.yaml) to create a named tunnel, store tunnel credentials JSON, create a cloudflared config.yml for the VPS. frontdoor_install_agent: SSH to VPS, install cloudflared deb package, write /etc/cloudflared/config.yml, enable and start cloudflared.service as a systemd unit. frontdoor_allowed_cidrs: fetch https://www.cloudflare.com/ips-v4 and https://www.cloudflare.com/ips-v6 and echo them (no local ports needed — tunnel is outbound-only). frontdoor_configure_dns: use Cloudflare API to create CNAME records routing *.<domain> to the tunnel hostname. frontdoor_connection_info: print instructions for using 'cloudflare access ssh' and the Zero Trust dashboard URL. Add bootstrap/providers/cloudflare-dns.sh as a shared helper for Cloudflare API calls (zone lookup, record create/update) reusable by both the front door and any provider that needs DNS. config.yaml.example cloudflare section needs: apiToken, accountId, tunnelName, zoneId.
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap/frontdoor/cloudflare.sh implements all 5 hooks
frontdoor_provision creates a Cloudflare Tunnel via API and writes tunnel credentials
frontdoor_install_agent installs cloudflared on the VPS as a systemd service via SSH
frontdoor_allowed_cidrs fetches live Cloudflare IP ranges (v4 + v6)
frontdoor_configure_dns creates wildcard CNAME via Cloudflare API
bootstrap/providers/cloudflare-dns.sh has reusable API helper functions
config.yaml.example has cloudflare.apiToken, accountId, tunnelName, zoneId
shellcheck passes on all new .sh files
After running: VPS has no open inbound ports, all traffic flows through tunnel

stories.title: Bootstrap: front door provider docs and custom provider template
stories.description: Add bootstrap/frontdoor/custom.sh.example as a heavily commented template for users implementing their own front door (WireGuard, Tailscale, custom reverse proxy, etc.) — each of the 5 hooks stubbed with comments describing exactly what it must do. Add docs/providers/cloudflare-setup.md covering: Cloudflare free account setup, creating an API token with Tunnel+DNS edit permissions, finding account ID and zone ID, Zero Trust SSH access setup, and how the bootstrap uses the tunnel. Add docs/providers/front-door-custom.md explaining the provider pattern, the 5 hooks contract, and a worked example implementing a WireGuard-based front door. Update docs/quickstart.md to include the front door selection step and security posture explanation (default: all ports closed, SSH via tunnel).
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap/frontdoor/custom.sh.example stubs all 5 hooks with detailed comments
docs/providers/cloudflare-setup.md covers full Cloudflare account and tunnel setup
docs/providers/front-door-custom.md explains the 5-hook contract with WireGuard example
docs/quickstart.md updated with front door selection and security posture section
All markdown files render without broken links or malformed headings

stories.title: Vendor manifest: VENDORS.yaml schema, license audit, and initial inventory
stories.description: Create the vendor manifest system — the 'ebuild database' of the platform. Create vendor/VENDORS.yaml defining every upstream project the platform depends on. Schema per entry: name, upstream_url, license_spdx, license_allows_vendor (bool — false for BSL/SSPL/AGPL), version_pinned, fetch_method (git_tag|tarball), distroless_compatible (yes|partial|no), distroless_base (gcr.io/distroless/static|java17|nodejs|none), deprecated (bool), deprecated_reason, alternative. Populate with ALL services in the current stack: cilium, crossplane, cert-manager, sealed-secrets, vault (flag BSL, alternative: openbao), keycloak, rook-ceph, gitlab, harbor, argocd, istio, opa-gatekeeper, trivy-operator, falco, prometheus-stack, loki, thanos, tempo, backstage, code-server, sonarqube, reportportal, selenium-grid, k6-operator, wiremock, mailhog, chaos-mesh. Create vendor/audit.sh that reads VENDORS.yaml and prints a report: license violations, distroless-incompatible services, deprecated entries with alternatives. Exit non-zero if any entry has license_allows_vendor: false without an alternative specified. NOTE: Vault must be marked license_allows_vendor: false with alternative: openbao. Update prd.json story 008 description to reference OpenBao (openbao/openbao) instead of HashiCorp Vault so the next iteration rebuilds that chart correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: vendor/VENDORS.yaml exists with all 27+ services from the current stack
Every entry has all required schema fields
Vault entry has license_allows_vendor: false and alternative: openbao
vendor/audit.sh produces a formatted report grouped by: license issues, distroless status, deprecated
vendor/audit.sh exits non-zero when any entry lacks an alternative for a non-vendorable license
shellcheck passes on vendor/audit.sh
prd.json story 008 updated to use OpenBao instead of HashiCorp Vault

stories.title: Vendor distroless audit: compatibility matrix, recipe stubs, and platform standard
stories.description: Create the definitive distroless compatibility audit. Create vendor/DISTROLESS.md as a living document with a table per vendor: service, language/runtime, distroless base image, status (compatible/partial/incompatible/rewrite-candidate), notes. Go-based services (cilium, cert-manager, crossplane, argocd, sealed-secrets, harbor, trivy-operator, openbao, thanos, loki, tempo, k6-operator) → distroless/static, mark compatible. Java-based (keycloak) → distroless/java21, mark partial with note suggesting Kanidm (Rust, native distroless) as long-term alternative. Node-based (backstage, code-server) → distroless/nodejs, mark partial. Shell-script containers → incompatible, note they run outside cluster. Create vendor/recipes/ directory with a recipe.yaml stub for each service — schema: name, upstream_url, version, license_spdx, fetch_method, distroless_base, build_tool (bazel|docker|skip). Update CLAUDE.md to add a DISTROLESS STANDARD section: all new container images must use distroless base; PRs adding non-distroless images require explicit deprecated entry in VENDORS.yaml with migration plan.
acceptanceCriteria: vendor/DISTROLESS.md has a row for every service in VENDORS.yaml
All Go-based services marked as distroless/static compatible
Keycloak marked partial with Kanidm noted as Rust alternative
vendor/recipes/ directory has recipe.yaml stub for every service
recipe.yaml schema has: name, upstream_url, version, license_spdx, fetch_method, distroless_base, build_tool
CLAUDE.md updated with DISTROLESS STANDARD section

stories.title: Vendor fetch framework: Gentoo-style source fetching into internal GitLab
stories.description: Build the source fetching framework. Equivalent to Gentoo portage fetch — gets upstream source into internal GitLab without creating runtime dependency on external git hosts. Create vendor/fetch.sh [name|all]: for each recipe in vendor/recipes/<name>/recipe.yaml, clone or update the upstream repo into the internal GitLab at gitlab.<domain>/vendor/<name> using GitLab API (GITLAB_TOKEN from env). Create the GitLab group 'vendor' and project if they don't exist, then mirror the upstream at the pinned version. Create vendor/update-check.sh: queries each upstream for new releases (GitHub API or git ls-remote), compares to pinned version in recipe.yaml, outputs report of available updates, optionally creates GitLab issues. Create vendor/pin.sh <name> <version>: updates version in recipe.yaml. Fetch method implementations: git_tag (clone at tag), tarball (download release tarball, verify SHA256 from upstream checksums file), git_commit (pin to SHA). Populate vendor/recipes/ with full recipe.yaml for the 10 most fundamental services: cilium, cert-manager, crossplane, argocd, sealed-secrets, openbao, keycloak, harbor, rook-ceph, prometheus-stack. All scripts must work with the GitLab instance running from story 014.
acceptanceCriteria: vendor/fetch.sh fetches a single named recipe or all recipes
vendor/fetch.sh creates GitLab group 'vendor' and project if not exists via API
vendor/fetch.sh mirrors upstream at pinned version into internal GitLab
vendor/update-check.sh reports available updates vs pinned versions
vendor/pin.sh <name> <version> updates recipe.yaml
vendor/recipes/ has full recipe.yaml for 10 fundamental services
vendor/fetch.sh --dry-run prints actions without making API calls
shellcheck passes on all vendor/*.sh

stories.title: Vendor Bazel workspace: distroless image build recipes for Go services
stories.description: Set up the Bazel build system for building distroless container images from vendored source. Create root MODULE.bazel (Bzlmod) and BUILD.bazel files. Use rules_go for Go compilation and rules_oci for OCI image assembly. Create bazel/toolchains/ with Go toolchain pinned to the version each service requires. Create bazel/distroless/ with reusable base image targets: distroless_static and distroless_java21. For each Go-based service, create vendor/recipes/<name>/BUILD.bazel that: compiles binary with rules_go, assembles distroless OCI image, tags for push to harbor.<domain>/sovereign/<name>:<version>. Implement BUILD.bazel for: cert-manager, crossplane, argocd, sealed-secrets, openbao. Create vendor/build.sh [name|all] that runs bazel build //vendor/recipes/<name>:image and pushes to Harbor using crane. Add .bazelrc with remote cache config. Builds must be reproducible: same source + same toolchain = bit-identical image. Create vendor/verify-distroless.sh that runs each built image with --version, confirms it exits cleanly, then attempts docker exec to verify no shell is present (must fail with 'not found').
acceptanceCriteria: MODULE.bazel and BUILD.bazel exist at repo root with rules_go and rules_oci
bazel/distroless/ has reusable distroless_static and distroless_java21 base targets
vendor/recipes/cert-manager/BUILD.bazel, crossplane/BUILD.bazel, argocd/BUILD.bazel, sealed-secrets/BUILD.bazel, openbao/BUILD.bazel all exist
vendor/build.sh builds and pushes to Harbor
vendor/verify-distroless.sh confirms no shell in built images
.bazelrc configures remote cache pointing to internal Harbor OCI cache
bazel query //vendor/recipes/...:all lists all image targets

stories.title: Vendor pipeline: GitLab CI triggers, ArgoCD Image Updater, Harbor-first Helm values
stories.description: Wire up the full vendor pipeline: source in GitLab → Bazel build in GitLab CI → push to Harbor → ArgoCD Image Updater detects new tag → ArgoCD syncs updated Helm release. Install ArgoCD Image Updater as an ArgoCD Application (argocd-apps/platform/argocd-image-updater-app.yaml) watching harbor.<domain>/sovereign/*. Create vendor/gitlab-ci-template.yml: on new tag pushed to internal mirror → bazel build → push harbor.<domain>/sovereign/<name>:<version> → create GitLab release. Update charts/_globals/values.yaml to add global.imageRegistry defaulting to harbor.<domain>/sovereign (empty string for bootstrap mode before Harbor is up). Update every chart's values.yaml image.repository to use global.imageRegistry prefix. Add ArgoCD Image Updater annotations to every ArgoCD Application manifest. Create vendor/smoke-test.sh: pulls each sovereign/* image from Harbor, runs --version, verifies image starts without a shell (distroless validation). This story completes the autarky loop: zero external image pulls after bootstrap.
acceptanceCriteria: ArgoCD Image Updater installed as ArgoCD Application with Harbor credentials
vendor/gitlab-ci-template.yml defines fetch→build→push pipeline
charts/_globals/values.yaml has global.imageRegistry
All chart values.yaml files use global.imageRegistry prefix
All ArgoCD Application manifests have argocd-image-updater annotations
vendor/smoke-test.sh validates each sovereign/* image is distroless
helm lint passes on all updated charts
End-to-end: pinning a new version in recipe.yaml → fetch → build → Harbor → ArgoCD auto-syncs

stories.title: Helm charts: Selenium Grid, k6, Wiremock, MailHog, Chaos Mesh
stories.description: Create 5 testing infrastructure charts: charts/selenium-grid/ (Selenium HQ official chart, Chrome+Firefox+Edge nodes), charts/k6-operator/ (grafana/k6-operator), charts/wiremock/ (wiremock/wiremock), charts/mailhog/ (mailhog deployment), charts/chaos-mesh/ (chaos-mesh/chaos-mesh). All get ArgoCD apps in argocd-apps/testing/. Selenium and MailHog get ingresses. Grafana dashboards for k6 results.

stories.title: Sovereign PM: Node.js/Express backend
stories.description: Create sovereign-pm/src/server/ — a Node.js + Express + TypeScript API server. Data model: Project, Epic, Story, AgentRun. PostgreSQL via Sequelize. Endpoints: CRUD for epics/stories, POST /api/prd/generate (outputs prd.json), POST /api/ralph/trigger (creates a Kubernetes Job running ralph.sh), GET /api/ralph/runs (list run history). Keycloak OIDC JWT validation middleware. OpenAPI spec in sovereign-pm/openapi.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: sovereign-pm/src/server/ TypeScript Express app
Sequelize models for Project, Epic, Story, AgentRun
All CRUD endpoints implemented
POST /api/prd/generate returns valid prd.json
POST /api/ralph/trigger creates a K8s Job
Keycloak JWT middleware on all routes
npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass
openapi.yaml documents all endpoints

stories.title: Sovereign PM: React frontend
stories.description: Create sovereign-pm/src/client/ — a React + TypeScript + Vite frontend. Pages: Dashboard (all projects), Project view (epics/stories kanban), Story editor (title, description, acceptance criteria, priority, phase), PRD Generator (shows generated prd.json, copy button), Agent Runs (history of Ralph loops, pass/fail per story, timestamps). Keycloak OIDC via keycloak-js. Tailwind CSS. Clean, minimal design.
acceptanceCriteria: React + TypeScript + Vite app in sovereign-pm/src/client/
All 5 pages implemented
Keycloak login flow works
PRD Generator shows formatted prd.json with copy button
Agent Runs page shows story pass/fail history
npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass
npm run build succeeds

stories.title: Sovereign PM: Dockerfile and Helm chart
stories.description: Create sovereign-pm/Dockerfile (multi-stage: build frontend, build backend, combined production image). Create charts/sovereign-pm/ Helm chart deploying the PM app. PostgreSQL dependency (or external Crossplane-managed). Keycloak OIDC config from values. Ingress at pm.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. Ralph scripts mounted as a volume (reads prd.json from PVC shared with the K8s Job). ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/devex/sovereign-pm-app.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile builds successfully (docker build .)

stories.title: Documentation: quickstart, architecture, and all provider guides
stories.description: Write docs/quickstart.md (15 minute path from zero to running cluster). Write docs/architecture.md (explains the bootstrap sequence, App-of-Apps pattern, namespace layout, security model, all services with their ingress URLs). Write docs/providers/aws-ec2-free-tier.md, docs/providers/digitalocean.md, docs/providers/vultr.md, docs/providers/bare-metal.md. Update README.md with badges, architecture diagram (ASCII), and contribution guide.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/quickstart.md covers: prerequisites, clone repo, fill config.yaml, run bootstrap.sh, verify
docs/architecture.md explains all phases and includes service URL table
All 4 provider docs exist with cost estimates and step-by-step instructions
README.md has ASCII architecture diagram showing service relationships
README.md links to all docs
All markdown lints clean (markdownlint)

stories.title: CI pipeline: lint and validate all Helm charts on every PR
stories.description: Create .github/workflows/validate.yml: on PR and push to main, run helm lint on all charts, helm template | kubectl apply --dry-run, shellcheck on all .sh files, markdownlint on all .md files. Create .github/workflows/release.yml: on tag push, package all Helm charts and publish to GitHub Pages as a Helm repository (so users can add sovereign as a Helm repo). Create charts/index.yaml structure for the Helm repo.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/validate.yml runs all lints on PR
.github/workflows/release.yml packages and publishes charts on tag
GitHub Pages Helm repository structure correct
All charts currently pass the lint workflow
README.md includes helm repo add sovereign instructions
